title: SINNERS
logline: In 1932 Mississippi, twin brothers return home to open a juke joint in an abandoned lumber mill, only to face a night of terror when vampires descend upon their grand opening, forcing them into a desperate fight for survival.
protagonist: Smoke and Stack (twin brothers), with Sammie as the emotional center
antagonistOrConflict: Remmick and a legion of vampires who attack the juke joint
tone: Visceral, atmospheric, and grounded—blending blues culture authenticity with supernatural dread
themes: The cost of ambition and escape
Music as spiritual power and cultural preservation
The violence of Jim Crow America meeting supernatural evil
Family bonds tested by impossible choices

actBreaks.description: The juke joint opens for business and the night begins—the setup is complete, the trap is set, and the story shifts from preparation to the unfolding nightmare.

validation.ruling: Scene #46 concludes the montage showing the mill's transformation into a functioning juke joint with all elements in place—this represents the completion of setup and the irreversible commitment to their venture, marking the transition from preparation to operation, though it's an unconventionally early act break at 39.4%.

actBreaks.description: Mary reveals herself as a vampire and the survivors barricade themselves inside—the conflict escalates from individual attack to full siege, fundamentally changing the stakes and strategy.

validation.ruling: Scene #86 is the definitive act break: Stack is dead, the supernatural threat is fully revealed, patrons debate leaving, Bo departs into darkness, and Cornbread returns oblivious to ask 'what happened to you?'—this irreversible loss and revelation fundamentally shifts the story from a business venture into a survival horror, forcing all characters into new, higher-stakes conflict they cannot escape.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a pressure-cooker siege narrative with an extended setup. Act One (scenes 1-46, roughly 50 pages) is deliberately patient, establishing the twins' return, their juke joint dream, the social fabric of 1932 Mississippi, and the gathering of characters who will face the night together. The prologue (scenes 1-7) establishes vampire mythology through Annie's voiceover and a farmhouse attack, then the script shifts to the twins' world-building for nearly 40 pages before the juke opens. Act Two (scenes 47-86, roughly 40 pages) is the escalating horror—the juke fills with patrons, Stack is attacked, Mary is revealed, and the siege begins. Act Three (scenes 87-110, roughly 37 pages) is pure survival horror: the group trapped inside, making impossible choices, losing members one by one, until Sammie's escape and the devastating epilogue 60 years later.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is bifurcated: a slow-burn first half building character and atmosphere (scenes 8-48 are almost entirely setup, montage, and character interaction), then a relentless second half once the vampires attack (scene 61 onward). The script spends significant time in the juke joint itself—scenes 46-110 all take place during one night at the lumber mill, creating claustrophobic intensity. The montage sequence (scenes 48-50) compresses the juke's transformation, then the script downshifts for character moments before the horror accelerates. The final 30 pages (scenes 87-110) are rapid-fire scene cuts between locations within the mill, creating frenetic energy as the survivors are picked off.

incitingIncident.description: Stack and Hogwood walk through the lumber mill while Smoke observes from upstairs; Smoke notices the floors have been washed and questions what was on them, then asserts they're grown men with grown money when Hogwood doubts their seriousness.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. LUMBER MILL - FIRST FLOOR - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #8 shatters the status quo by establishing Smoke and Stack's purchase of the lumber mill with cash and threats, forcing them into irreversible action in Mississippi. This transaction introduces the main conflict (establishing their juke joint in hostile territory), sets up stakes with their warning to Hogwood about killing trespassers, and occurs at 7.1% which is slightly early but acceptable for an unconventional structure.

midpoint.description: Cornbread notices the three drifters approaching and sends someone to get the twins, Remmick asks to come in claiming they're musicians who are hungry, Cornbread hesitates, the Twins arrive with others, and Remmick locks eyes with Sammie.

validation.ruling: Scene #61 is merely a sexual encounter with no structural significance. Scene #64 (p67, 53%) is the true midpoint: the three white drifters arrive at the mill seeking entry, Cornbread sends for the twins, and Remmick locks eyes with Sammie—this is the point of no return that shifts the story from reactive business operations to proactive survival against supernatural threat, raising stakes from financial success to mortal danger.

climax.description: Remmick pursues and catches Sammie at the river, strikes him with claws, pulls him from the water by his throat, and declares his intent to take Sammie's stories and songs.

validation.ruling: Scene #103 is the penultimate confrontation between brothers, not the ultimate obstacle. Scene #104 (p115, 91%) is the true climax: Remmick catches Sammie at the river, strikes him with claws, pulls him from the water by his throat, and declares his intent to take Sammie's stories and songs—this is the peak confrontation with the primary antagonist at maximum tension, occurring at the optimal climactic position.

scenes.heading: INT. DARK VOID - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A match strikes three times and ignites, illuminating a woodcut image of a guitarist while Annie's voiceover describes legends of musicians who can pierce the veil between life and death, conjuring spirits.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: An injured man hobbles toward a farmhouse at sunset, looking back toward an unseen pursuer.

scenes.heading: INT. FARMHOUSE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Joan serves Bert a bowl of liquid meant to increase his count; he refuses it, they banter about Aunt Comfort's fertility remedy, then someone knocks loudly on the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - SUNSET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A search party led by Chayton arrives at the farmhouse as the sun sets, and Chayton knocks on the door.

scenes.heading: INT. FARMHOUSE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Joan answers the door with a rifle and denies seeing anyone; Chayton senses her dishonesty and warns her that the person they're pursuing is dangerous, but Joan cocks her rifle and tells them to leave.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSERY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joan finds the injured man standing in the dark nursery; he reveals Bert's lifeless body in a pool of blood, licks his palm, and grins wider as Joan begins to cry.
scenes.turningPointNote: First explicit supernatural violence — the injured man has killed Bert and reveals himself as a threat

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - DAY
scenes.summary: Twin brothers Smoke and Stack wait beside a parked car at an abandoned lumber mill, sharing a cigarette as a Packard approaches in the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. LUMBER MILL - FIRST FLOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Stack and Hogwood walk through the lumber mill while Smoke observes from upstairs; Smoke notices the floors have been washed and questions what was on them, then asserts they're grown men with grown money when Hogwood doubts their seriousness.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREY OAKS PLANTATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie works barefoot in a cotton field at dawn, humming a tune; another sharecropper asks where he's playing guitar tonight, and Sammie smiles but doesn't answer.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOORE SHACK - SHARECROPPERS QUARTERS’ - GREY OAKS
scenes.summary: Sammie approaches his mother Ruthie doing laundry, trades his sweaty shirt for a clean one, and uses her soapy water to wash despite her protests.

scenes.heading: INT. MOORE SHACK - SHARECROPPERS QUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie enters the one-room shack where his five younger siblings sleep and searches for something but doesn't find it.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOORE SHACK - SHARECROPPERS’ QUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie exits and tells Ruthie his guitar and slide are missing; Ruthie gives him a knowing glance that tells him where they are.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHURCH - GREY OAKS PLANTATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie approaches the church with an angered gait but calms himself before entering.

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH - GREY OAKS PLANTATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie finds his father Jedidiah reading the Bible with Sammie's guitar and slide at the pulpit; Jedidiah wants Sammie to lead the children in a church song, but Sammie plays a blues version instead, then takes his guitar and leaves.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHURCH - GREY OAKS PLANTATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie walks toward Smoke and Stack's waiting car while Jedidiah watches; Stack tells Jedidiah they'll bring Sammie back in one piece as they drive away.

scenes.heading: I/E. SMOKE AND STACK’S SEDAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke drives with Stack and Sammie; they ask about Sammie's wellbeing and his father, then Sammie asks about Chicago and the twins describe the city and its cold weather.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE OF THE ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The three pull foliage off a hidden truck full of supplies; a timber rattlesnake appears on the truck bed near Sammie, and Smoke kills it with a knife thrown by Stack.

scenes.heading: INT. STACK’S SEDAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Stack drives and asks Sammie about his relationship with Jedidiah; Sammie asks if the twins really killed their father, and Stack reveals that Smoke killed him after their father knocked Stack unconscious.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major character revelation — Smoke killed his own father to protect Stack

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREE T - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke parks downtown, calls over a teenager, identifies himself as one of the Smokestack Twins, and pays her to watch his truck and honk the horn if anyone looks at it too long.

scenes.heading: INT. DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke enters the grocery store and greets Bo Chow; he orders catfish, collards, and other food for fifty to one hundred people for an event tonight.

scenes.heading: EXT. DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke finds two thieves stealing from his truck; he shoots one in the buttocks, aims at the other who stops running, then recognizes them as Terry and realizes they know each other.

scenes.heading: INT. DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke gives Bo money to get the thieves patched up, orders ice, and asks if Grace can make signs for tonight; Bo agrees and sends Lisa to get Grace.

scenes.heading: EXT. DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lisa walks across the street to another Delta Grocery Store and enters.

scenes.heading: INT. OTHER DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lisa tells her mother Grace that her father wants her; Grace finishes with a customer and heads for the exit.

scenes.heading: EXT. DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace walks across the street, sees Smoke's truck and the two wounded thieves, then enters the store.

scenes.heading: INT. DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grace confronts Smoke about bringing trouble; Smoke shows cash and asks for two signs by tonight; Grace demands twenty dollars, Smoke counters with ten, and they settle on twelve dollars and fifty cents.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAILROAD STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Stack and Sammie arrive at the bustling railroad station where black musicians play for coins; they find Delta Slim playing incredible harmonica, and Stack gives him a dollar.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAILROAD STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie and Delta Slim perform a guitar and harmonica duet for a crowd; Stack announces they'll be playing tonight at the old sawmill and describes the event as a battle of the blues with food, drink, and gambling.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANNIE’S SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke visits a tiny grave with a painted stone and child's handprint, places flowers beside it, then Annie emerges from her shack and they discuss why he's returned.

scenes.heading: INT. ANNIE'S SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside Annie's shop, young girls buy pickled pig feet and High John dust with plantation currency; Smoke criticizes Annie for accepting the fake money, and she explains people have nothing else and she can trade it.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIS SISSIPPI ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Stack drives with Delta Slim and Sammie; Sammie asks Stack about performing oral sex on the woman at the station, and Delta Slim encourages Stack to educate Sammie about treating women.

scenes.heading: INT. STACK’S SEDAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Delta Slim's smile fades as he reveals he knew all the men on the chain gang; he tells the story of being arrested for vagrancy with his friend Rice and spending a month in jail.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAYBELL PLANTATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Stack stops to recruit Cornbread from the plantation; when Cornbread says he's behind on his quota, Stack curses his quota and his wife, then offers him a chance to make real money tonight.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISSISSIPPI ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Stack drives with Delta Slim, Cornbread, and Sammie as a montage begins.

scenes.heading: EXT. DELTA GROCERY STORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bo and Grace Chow load groceries, paint supplies, and wood while Lisa and her grandmother watch from the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Smoke and Stack arrive at the lumber mill with black workers; Stack watches Smoke help Annie down from the truck, and they unload liquor, beer, groceries, lumber, a piano, and rifles.

scenes.heading: INT. LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Smoke, Stack, Sammie, Cornbread, Annie, and Slim load equipment into the mill.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Sammie siphons water from the river.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Sammie brings excess alcohol into the back room.

scenes.heading: INT. FLOOR - LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Sammie walks through the mill with wide eyes, taking in the bar, gambling area, and dance floor.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: A labor party arrives by mule and wagon; Smoke guides them where to park while Annie watches vultures circle overhead.

scenes.heading: INT. FLOOR - LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Sammie and Delta Slim help the twins nail together a small stage.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: The Chows arrive and Grace begins preparing the sign after discussing placement with the twins.

scenes.heading: I/E. LUMBER MILL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Annie and black women unload and cook food while Stack and workers cover gaps in walls; fish is fried and sandwiches are made.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: As the sun sets, the twins instruct Cornbread on working the door, Smoke taps electricity from a pole, and Sammie places a washtub at the entrance.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The lights turn on, revealing the transformed mill with food cooking and musicians preparing; Smoke stands calculating while Stack slaps his back, beaming.
scenes.turningPointNote: The juke joint is complete and ready to open — the plan is now in motion

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT- LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie helps Slim tune the piano; Slim tells Sammie that the same spirit flows through blues and church music, just in a more honest way, and that blues wasn't forced on them but came from within.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The first patron enters the empty juke joint and orders corn whiskey at the bar, saying he'll wait until they start playing.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the back room, Smoke insists they only accept US dollars, but Annie and Stack argue that sharecroppers need to feel wanted and should be allowed to use plantation currency.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - JUKE JOINT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie listens from the hallway as Smoke argues that accepting plantation currency is bad for business.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - JUKE JOINT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stack tells Annie they'll let patrons pay with what they have on opening night, Smoke storms out needing a cigarette, and Annie exits without returning Stack's smile.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL
scenes.summary: Smoke tells Sammie to play well tonight, a patron praises the corn liquor, more patrons arrive, and Delta Slim prepares to perform.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Folks from around town walk into the lumber mill with Cornbread checking them in.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Delta Slim performs a vocal blues song from behind the piano.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie brings Cornbread a beer, Pearline arrives and questions when the place became a juke, and she's surprised to learn Sammie is blood cousins with the Twins.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cornbread lets in a group of young women, then is startled when a white woman named Mary approaches and he recognizes her as someone he knew years ago.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie and Pearline flirt about performing and her older church-type husband, then Sammie spots Mary and tries to make her leave, warning she upset Stack, but Mary refuses and tells him to get out of her face.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Delta Slim performs on stage while Smoke points out Mary to Stack, who confronts her and tries to pay her to leave, but Mary refuses and laughs when Stack threatens to have someone drag her out.

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE FLOOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Delta Slim introduces Sammie from the stage, and Sammie makes his way to the middle of the dance floor with his guitar, surrounded by clapping patrons.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Three white drifters—Remmick, Joan, and Bert—walk toward the mill carrying instruments and a lantern, with Remmick looking hungrily at the party.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROO M - DAY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Sammie and Pearline enter the back room and make out heavily, Pearline removes her panties and sits on a table, then Sammie goes down on her despite her initial protest about not washing up.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stack listens at the door and chuckles silently before Grace tells him Smoke is asking for him upstairs, then Grace listens at the door and makes a face.

scenes.heading: INT. CATWALK - UPPER LEVEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stack finds Smoke on the catwalk observing the crowd, Smoke shows Stack the till count revealing they made plantation money on opening night, then reveals the bottom figure is what they owe Hogwood.

scenes.heading: EXT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL
scenes.summary: Cornbread notices the three drifters approaching and sends someone to get the twins, Remmick asks to come in claiming they're musicians who are hungry, Cornbread hesitates, the Twins arrive with others, and Remmick locks eyes with Sammie.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Twins, Cornbread, Annie, and Mary huddle to discuss the white visitors, Smoke worries about Hogwood setting them up and regrets not getting the trunk, Stack doubts the connection, and Mary offers to check if they're good people.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL
scenes.summary: Mary grabs her scarf, checks the locations of Annie, Smoke, and Cornbread, then slips out through the barn door into the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary approaches Remmick's group as they play music, asks if they're good people with money to spend, Bert shows her gold coins from a different land and time, and Remmick tells her she can have one if she gets them inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary returns from the shadows and tells Cornbread she convinced the white folks to leave, Cornbread sees their lantern shrinking in the distance and thanks her, then lets her back inside.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL
scenes.summary: Mary moves through the partying crowd, Pearline takes the stage with a guitarist and begins performing, and Cornbread notices Delta Slim heading outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cornbread walks away from the mill with a lantern to relieve himself, hears rustling and checks both sides nervously, then Joan appears holding his lantern and tells him not to stop on her account.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL
scenes.summary: Stack finds Mary coming from around a corner wiping her mouth, she shows him the gold coin, he asks for his pistol back and notices it's light, then Mary kisses him before he can check it.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - JUKE JOINT - NIGHT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Stack and Mary kiss passionately in the back room, she licks his neck, they begin having sex on the floor, and Stack notices drool trailing from her mouth which she offers to share with him.

scenes.heading: EXT. HALLWAY - JUKE JOINT - NIGHT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Smoke pulls Sammie aside and praises his performance, warns him the musician's life gets old, then tells him about Mound Bayou and offers to set him up there with money and a car instead of letting him ramble.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMBLING TABLES - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two gamblers are in an altercation over loaded dice, one holds a straight razor to the other's bleeding face, Smoke tries to de-escalate but the gambler refuses to put down the blade and threatens to cut Smoke too.

scenes.heading: INT. B ACK ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie sees Stack and Mary having sex on the ground, quickly shuts the door and smiles at Delta Slim who laughs, then tells Smoke he couldn't get Stack because he was getting nookie.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Smoke enters the back room to find Mary slurping blood from Stack's jugular vein, Mary looks up with blood on her mouth and face, then Smoke shoots her three times in the chest.
scenes.turningPointNote: The supernatural threat is revealed — Mary is a vampire who has attacked Stack, triggering the horror conflict and Smoke's violent response.

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE FLOOR - JUKE JOINT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Pearline, the band, and dancing patrons hear gunshots and look around in confusion.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMBLING TABLES - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Annie hears the gunshot, turns toward the back room, tells Bo to wait, then rushes out of the gambling room.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL
scenes.summary: Delta Slim turns and looks toward the back room.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary drops from the gunshots but laughs almost unaffected, rises up with gunshot wounds, declares they'll kill everyone, then charges past Smoke avoiding more shots and runs out.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary sprints past Delta Slim with her chest and face bloodied, tells him she'll be right back and they're going to kill everyone and have real fun tonight, then Delta Slim runs toward the back room.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Delta Slim finds Smoke cradling Stack and applying pressure to the wound, Stack's body convulses then goes still with empty eyes, Annie enters and checks for a pulse finding none, then asks what happened.
scenes.turningPointNote: Stack dies from Mary's attack — a devastating loss that confirms the lethal vampire threat and propels the survivors into crisis mode.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Delta Slim keeps patrons back from the back room, they ask what's happening and if the music is still going, Delta Slim announces the party's over, and confused patrons start filing out muttering.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK ROOM - JUKE J OINT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie blames himself for not stopping it, Annie asks if Mary said anything, Smoke reports she said 'we gonna kill all of you,' Annie urgently insists they move Stack's body outside, and Smoke refuses violently when Annie reaches for the body.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Smoke emerges covered in Stack's blood with Annie and Sammie, they lock the back room door from outside, Smoke tells Bo that Stack is dead, Annie tells Sammie not to let anyone in, then she follows Smoke and the others.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT -
scenes.summary: Pearline tells Sammie she's scared to go outside and he says she can stay, Annie warns everyone not to go outside because it's not safe, patrons debate and leave anyway, Grace wants to leave but Bo insists on staying with Smoke, then Bo heads out to get the car while Cornbread returns from the darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Someone knocks gently on the back room door from inside, Sammie and Pearline are rattled, a voice whispers from inside, then Stack's knife blade plunges through the door nearly hitting Sammie's eye before being yanked back with Stack's familiar laugh heard.
scenes.turningPointNote: Stack has turned into a vampire and is now trapped inside the back room — confirming the infection spreads and the dead return as threats.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL
scenes.summary: Grace argues the group must go get Bo who's outside, Annie refuses to let her go, Smoke says Bo can handle himself, Annie lists what they need to fight vampires, and explains vampires are different from haints because they take over the actual person.

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE FLOOR - JUKE JOINT - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie, Smoke, and Delta Slim break down wood into stakes, Annie, Grace, and Pearline gather garlic, Annie throws bones and reads them with distress, then asks Smoke to promise he'll kill her if she gets bitten before she turns.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie and Smoke drag Gambler 2's body outside, Sammie sees a juke patron walking off to join a massive group of former patrons in the distance, then Sammie hears music playing and Smoke pulls him back inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Remmick performs a solo in the center of concentric circles of vampiric juke patrons including Stack, Mary, and Cornbread who dance while Bert, Joan, and other musicians play furiously.
scenes.turningPointNote: The full scope of the vampire legion is revealed — nearly all the juke patrons have been turned and are now under Remmick's control, surrounding the mill.

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE FLOOR - JUKE JOINT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Annie has everyone eat a clove of garlic to test if anyone is a vampire, Annie, Smoke, Sammie, and Grace eat theirs, but Pearline hesitates calling it ridiculous, Smoke lifts his pistol, Sammie tells him to put it down, and Pearline finally eats the garlic.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gambler 2 knocks on the front door calling for Smoke to let him in and beats on the door.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRY HALLWAY - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Smoke and Sammie run to the door with the others behind them, and Smoke opens it.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cornbread tackles Gambler 2 and bites his neck, Grace sees Bo approaching who acts like the attack is normal, Bo reveals he's turned by smiling and asking Grace to come out or let him in, then Remmick appears offering to be their way out and promising fellowship.

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE FLOOR - LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The survivors gather their weapons, Grace is in shock that Bo threatened their daughter, Annie says he was lying to get her to open the door, but Grace insists they must stop the vampires before they kill the whole town.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Remmick leads the vampires including Bo, Cornbread, Stack, and Mary in singing as they surround the mill, with Bo circling to a window to sing directly to Grace while making eye contact.

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE FLOOR - JUKE JOINT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grace covers her ears, grabs a stake and walks to the window, Smoke and Sammie restrain her covering her mouth, she bites Smoke's hand and breaks free with help from others piling on, then yells for the vampires to come inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. DANCE FLOOR - JUKE JOINT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. CATWALK - UPPER LEVEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Remmick blocks the balcony escape route and attacks Sammie while Stack tackles Smoke; Pearline stabs Remmick in the back but he turns and bites her neck.
scenes.turningPointNote: Pearline is bitten and turned by Remmick; Stack attacks his own brother Smoke, forcing the twins into direct violent conflict

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - RIVERSIDE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sammie lands hard outside the mill and limps toward the river.

scenes.heading: INT. CATWALK - UPPER LEVEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stack overpowers Smoke in a brutal fight, throws the newly-turned Pearline off the balcony, mounts Smoke and tries to convince him to accept vampirism, but stops his fangs just before biting.
scenes.turningPointNote: Stack's attempt to turn Smoke reaches its climax but he hesitates at the final moment, revealing internal conflict about his choice

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Remmick pursues and catches Sammie at the river, strikes him with claws, pulls him from the water by his throat, and declares his intent to take Sammie's stories and songs.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke tells Sammie to go home and bury the guitar; Sammie reveals Stack claimed it was Charlie Patton's, but Smoke corrects him that it belonged to their father.

scenes.heading: EXT. LUMBER MILL - DAY
scenes.summary: Smoke ambushes Hogwood's crew with the Browning machine gun, kills multiple henchmen including Hogwood, takes a bullet to the belly, then destroys an escaping truck with a grenade.
scenes.turningPointNote: Smoke eliminates Hogwood and his entire crew in a decisive massacre, ending that threat permanently despite being wounded

scenes.heading: EXT. CHURCH HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie parks at the church and walks to the door, still blood-stained and dazed.

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie enters the church during a children's choir performance and his father Jedidiah extends his arm from the pulpit, causing Sammie to cry.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sammie returns to his father and the church after the night's horrors, marking his emotional reconciliation and survival

scenes.heading: EXT. MISSISSIPPI STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Sammie drives Stack's car down the road, pressing the guitar neck to his heart as grief overwhelms him.

scenes.heading: INT. PEARLINE’S BLUES BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: An 80-year-old Sammie performs at Pearline's Blues Bar in Chicago in 1992; after closing, a bouncer announces two late arrivals offering money to enter, and a familiar voice thanks him, causing Sammie to freeze mid-drink.
scenes.turningPointNote: The epilogue reveals Sammie's survival and success decades later, then introduces a mysterious familiar presence that suggests the vampire threat may have returned
